title: Fracture / Soft-Wrist Assembly
description: Keep in Survey: fracture reassembly and soft-wrist insertion each have strong references, but the first benchmark choice is still open.
summary: Survey-stage track for Notion child pages on fractured-object reassembly and soft-wrist contact-rich policy: combine geometric assembly, ordering/planning, force/tactile feedback, and compliant hardware into a possible manipulation benchmark.

details.text: Stepwise fractured-object reassembly idea: use one arm to predict ordered trajectories for fractured parts. Breaking Bad provides a large geometric fracture/reassembly benchmark, but the robotics gap is sequencing, grasping, collision, and physical verification rather than only part-pose prediction.

details: Notion extensions: bimanual reassembly, image guidance, graph networks, and data generation through disassembly. These are survey-level until there is a physical object set and a simulator/real evaluation protocol.

details.text: Soft Wrist Contact-rich Policy idea: soft wrist insertion suggests a robust route for peg/hole and uncertain contact tasks. Its best connection to the dashboard is a tactile/force assembly benchmark, not a standalone robot-hardware build.

details: Open Notion items folded here: peg CAD plus force sensor contact localization, general contact-rich assembly policy, and using compliant hardware as either a training simplifier or a real-world safety margin.

details.text: Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: Stepwise fractured-object reassembly, Soft Wrist Contact-rich Policy, peg CAD + force-sensor collision localization, Tactile-Bolt, and screw/cap/bolt articulated-object manipulation are connected as one assembly/contact-rich benchmark family, with VTLA owning sensor representation details.

risks_decisions: Fracture reassembly and soft-wrist insertion may be two papers; keep one survey card only until a shared benchmark emerges.
Synthetic geometry success does not imply robot success. Need graspability, collision, force, fixture, and object-damage constraints.
A soft wrist can hide controller weaknesses if used only as compliance; define what perception/control signal is actually learned.
First deliverable should choose between fractured-object sequencing and peg/contact insertion as the lower-risk first benchmark.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because Breaking Bad and soft-wrist insertion solve different components; they do not decide whether our first assembly benchmark should be fractured-object reassembly or compliant insertion.
Next executable analysis: choose one benchmark family, list assets and sensors, define simulator/real split, and write success/failure metrics before opening engineering TODOs.
Archive only if both candidate benchmark families are rejected as too far from current hardware and dataset goals.

Tactile-Bolt appears in both VTLA and this assembly card by design: VTLA decides the sensing/data representation, while this card decides whether screw/bolt/insertion/reassembly is the first benchmark.
